operating_state: Total revenue increased 15% to $44.8 billion driven by comparable sales growth of 13% and 18 net new warehouses opened since prior year. Core merchandise categories experienced broad-based strength particularly in fresh foods, while ancillary businesses including gasoline and travel weakened significantly due to COVID-19 impacts. E-commerce comparable sales surged 76% contributing to overall performance despite gasoline price declines. Membership renewal rates remained strong at 91% in the U.S. and Canada and 88% worldwide.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased 2 basis points to 10.96% as core merchandise improvement was offset by ancillary business declines. SG&A expenses increased 11 basis points as percentage of sales due to incremental COVID-19 wages, partially offset by operational leverage from higher sales volumes. Company implemented permanent wage increases effective March 2021 with estimated annualized pre-tax impact after ending COVID-related incremental wages totaling approximately $825 million since March 2020.
material_events: Board declared quarterly dividend of $0.70 per share and special dividend of $10.00 per share totaling $4.4 billion paid December 2020. Company acquired Innovel Solutions for $998 million in March 2020, now operating as Costco Wholesale Logistics for final-mile delivery capabilities. Stock repurchases totaled $189 million in first half with remaining authorization available.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 continues impacting operations with supply constraints affecting merchandise categories expected to persist and negative effects on gasoline and travel ancillary businesses. Tariff actions across multiple jurisdictions create uncertainty regarding merchandise costs dependent on goods type and timing. Foreign currency changes positively impacted net sales primarily from Canadian and Other International operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects to open 13 additional warehouses in remainder of fiscal 2021 with capital expenditures planned for full year. Company maintains strong liquidity with $9.3 billion in cash and short-term investments expecting operating cash flows to meet foreseeable capital requirements. COVID-related supply constraints expected to continue affecting merchandise categories.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple California labor law class actions pending regarding wage and hour practices with settlements reached in some cases, while federal securities litigation dismissed with appeal pending. Construction project timelines remain subject to COVID-19 related delays. Bank credit facilities provide additional liquidity with most facilities requiring renewal within one year.
